benchmark.images: 4 photographs — sticky notes (pink, yellow, green, manila) + spiral notepad
benchmark.glyph_map_version: v0.2 (AI #2 used v0.2 with DecipherKit protocol; others used raw vision)
benchmark.note: User submitted results without labels. AI identities to be guessed at end.

ai_1.name: AI #1
ai_1.label: Blind — identity unknown
notable_successes: Cross-referenced Siri note and VibeForge across images
Color ID better than Claude's first pass (got green right)
notable_failures: A Full Siri Record APP → 'A few siri recos' (two words wrong)
OpenClaw → 0 for 4 (read as 'Open cal', 'Open C/L', 'Open C/L UI')
Token Usage → inconsistent (wrote 'Ticket usage' then 'Token' later)
hallucinations: 'Voice control' on pink sticky edge — invented
'Forge Embed Code' on green sticky — fabricated plausible tech term

ai_2.name: AI #2 (Claude with DecipherKit v0.2)
ai_2.label: Blind — identity unknown (but clearly has glyph map)
notable_successes: Color accuracy → correct (3 pink, 3 yellow/green, 1 white)
Date flagged as future (smart contextual observation)
notable_failures: A Full Siri Record APP → 'A Full Suite APP' (dropped word, changed another)
'— Siri —' context bleed into notepad (was '— gives —')
Surface 8 flagged as unreadable (was '← are we connected?')
hallucinations: '— Siri —' context bleed from yellow sticky into notepad
ai_2.key_insight: Glyph map is double-edged sword. Fixed Rep Plan and Tracks but caused context bleed (Siri → notepad). Known vocabulary matching needs confidence gate.

ai_3.name: AI #3 (Cautious)
ai_3.label: Blind — identity unknown
notable_successes: Zero hallucinations — only AI to achieve this
Honest about uncertainty — flagged low confidence reads
notable_failures: Lowest raw accuracy of all AIs
Notepad right column mostly wrong after 'Center'
ai_3.key_insight: Proves that conservative reading with honest uncertainty flags is safer than confident wrong answers. Would be the best foundation for a correction loop — clean data in, learn fast.

ai_4.name: AI #4 (Hype Machine)
ai_4.label: Blind — identity unknown
ai_4.note: Some hallucinations overlap with wrong reads (e.g., 'Video Forge study' is both wrong AND a hallucination). Hallucinated count includes fabricated content that doesn't correspond to any real surface element.
notable_successes: Got '(Audit)' right — only second AI to do so
notable_failures: Called user 'Jethro' — name pulled from somewhere or hallucinated
Built entire theme ('Connectivity + media') on fabricated 'Video' reads
Listed hallucinated words as action items ('Verifon' as a clear task)
HIGH confidence on wrong reads ('Video Forge study' marked HIGH)
Got date AND time wrong (04/18/26 should be 04/19/26, 1:52 should be 1:53)
'Claude CO VOR K' instead of 'Claude Cowork'
hallucinations: 'Video' on pink sticky edge — repeated 3 times, never exists (CRITICAL: built entire theme on it)
'Verifon' in notepad — fabricated, then listed as action item (CRITICAL)
'movie com?' — misread then left as speculation
ai_4.key_insight: Most dangerous output for production. Reads like the most confident and polished response, but worst accuracy-to-confidence ratio. A user without the baseline would trust this most and be wrong most. Confident wrong answers are worse than honest uncertain ones.

procurement_os.note: 0 for 4. Every single AI missed this. Edge scanning is a universal failure mode.

openclaw_on_notepad.surface: S10 — notepad left column line 1 and right column line 2
openclaw_on_notepad.note: OpenClaw appears 6+ times across all images. No AI consistently identified it. This is the #1 candidate for glyph map correction value.

engineering_actions.action: Add confidence gate to glyph map
engineering_actions.rationale: Vocab matching only fires on genuinely ambiguous letterforms
ALL four AIs missed Procurement OS at image edge
'— gives —' is a section divider pattern not previously documented
Surface 8 was resolvable but AI #2 flagged it as unreadable

AI #4 shows that confident hallucinations (Verifon as action item, Video as theme basis) are more dangerous than low-confidence misreads. Need a 3-tier severity: fabrication (invented data), context bleed (real word wrong place), misread (wrong letterform interpretation).

AI #4 marked wrong reads as HIGH confidence. DecipherKit output should include a calibration score: how well does stated confidence match actual accuracy? Poorly calibrated confidence is worse than no confidence.

engineering_actions.action: OpenClaw as mandatory glyph map test word
engineering_actions.rationale: Universal blind spot — 0 for 4 AIs on notepad. 'Open' + 'Claw' combination consistently misread as 'Open cal', 'Open Claude', 'Operations'. Needs explicit letterform training in glyph map.

key_findings: No AI achieved above 54% accuracy on personalized handwriting — proves the market need for DecipherKit
The glyph map (AI #2) provided the highest accuracy but introduced context bleed — confidence gate is essential
Zero AIs got Procurement OS — edge scanning is a universal gap
OpenClaw is the universal blind spot — appears 6+ times, misread by every AI differently
AI #3's zero-hallucination approach is the safest foundation for a correction loop
AI #4 proves that confidence without accuracy is the most dangerous output mode — DecipherKit must prevent this
The notepad (structured, multi-column handwriting) is dramatically harder than sticky notes (large, isolated text) for all AIs
Structural notation (dashes, arrows, parentheses) was missed or misinterpreted by 3 of 4 AIs
product_implications: DecipherKit v0.4 should combine AI #2's glyph map accuracy with AI #3's hallucination-free approach
Confidence calibration metric needed — users must know when to trust output and when to verify
Correction loop is the real product moat — each correction makes the next read better
Multi-surface synthesis (reading across images) is a differentiator no raw AI does well
Market positioning: 'Every AI we tested gets your handwriting wrong half the time. DecipherKit learns YOUR writing.'
